1.tts_text: When you visit a website and see content like paragraphs, headings, links, images, and videos, that's HTML.

12.tts_text: Both opening and closing tags start with a left angle bracket, and end with a right angle bracket, with the tag name placed between these angle brackets.

14.tts_text: Here is a closer look at just the opening and closing paragraph tags.

15.tts_text: What distinguishes an opening tag from a closing tag is the forward slash placed immediately after the left angle bracket in a closing tag.

18.tts_text: Here is an example of an image element which is a void element.

21.tts_text: Sometimes you will see void elements that use a slash before the right angle bracket like this.

22.tts_text: While many code formatters like Prettier, will choose to include the slash in void elements, the HTML spec states that the presence of the slash "does not mark the start tag as self-closing but instead is unnecessary and has no effect of any kind".

27.tts_text: Update the value of the src attribute to the specified URL and you will see the image change to two cats peacefully sleeping.

32.tts_text: Try breaking the image by updating the src value to the specified URL.

35.tts_text: Well, the answer is, it depends.
